events.label: assistant
events.text: I’ll create the required JSON directly at `/app/answer.json`. This is a sequencing call: pad wear indicator contact means the brake friction material is already in a defect/worn state, so active work should not proceed past diagnosis without inspection and corrective brake service per OEM procedure.

events.label: shell
events.text:
/bin/sh -lc "pwd && ls -la && printf '%s
' '{
  \"decision\": \"fail\",
  \"risk\": \"high\",
  \"work_stage\": \"in-progress\",
  \"component_conditions\": [\"installed-defective\", \"worn\"],
  \"percent_complete\": 25,
  \"value\": 0,
  \"sound_source\": \"pad wear indicator contact at brake pad/rotor interface\",
  \"confidence\": 0.86,
  \"event_time\": 0.0,
  \"rate\": 0.0,
  \"order\": [\"verify-symptom\", \"secure-vehicle\", \"inspect-pad-thickness\", \"inspect-rotor-condition\", \"inspect-caliper-hardware\", \"compare-to-oem-specs\", \"replace-worn-defective-brake-components\", \"bed-in-or-service-per-oem\", \"road-test-and-final-brake-inspection\"],
  \"workable\": [],
  \"findings\": [\"Pad wear indicator contact indicates brake pads are at or below service limit.\", \"Continuing operation or unrelated work before brake inspection and correction risks rotor damage and reduced braking performance.\", \"Installed brake component is defective/worn and must be treated as a safety-critical condition.\"],
  \"actions\": [\"Stop the current sequence before continuing service work.\", \"Perform OEM brake inspection including pad thickness, rotor condition, caliper operation, hardware, and any electronic wear sensor circuit if equipped.\", \"Replace worn pads and any damaged or out-of-spec rotors/hardware before returning the asset to service.\", \"Complete brake function test, burnish/bed-in if required by OEM, and final inspection before acceptance.\"],
  \"rationale\": \"Pad wear indicator contact is not a benign in-progress condition; it is evidence that the installed brake friction material has reached the wear limit. Before work can continue or the vehicle can be returned to service, the sequence must move back to safety control, OEM inspection, correction of worn brake components, and post-repair brake testing.\",
  \"references\": [\"OEM brake service specifications\", \"FMVSS-adjacent shop practice for safety-critical brake inspection and functional verification\"]
}' > /app/answer.json
cat /app/answer.json"
→ /app
total 8
